newsSinceJun6: NOK 300m subsequent offering settled: 11.1m new shares at NOK 27 issued/delivered on or about June 8, oversubscribed ~2.6x (29.4m shares demanded), with CEO Helge Aasen, CFO Morten Viga and board members participating. Today's +3.55% to NOK 33.26 looks flow-driven - dilution overhang cleared and stock trades well above the NOK 27 offer price - not a fundamental rerating. No new contracts, guidance or analyst moves since June 6. Standing context: June 3 Statkraft 7-year PPA (1,534 GWh, 2031-2037) covers the Bjolvefossen foundry-alloys plant (not AI-relevant), and the pending majority sale of the Silicones division to Bluestar (agreed Feb 2026, EGM-approved Mar 9) continues to shrink the silicones-based AI-spillover pathway but is not new information.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Elkem down for the rank/score gap, and nothing genuinely new has hit since June 6 - the offering settlement and today's +3.55% are flow events, not evidence for the latent silicones/carbon AI pathway. Small negative drift because the pending Bluestar silicones divestiture keeps hollowing out the very division this page's thesis leans on.
verdicts.bull: Insiders (CEO, CFO, board) bought stock at NOK 27 in an oversubscribed raise, the balance sheet is repaired, and a slimmed-down silicon/carbon materials pure-play at a $1.3B cap is cheap optionality if AI thermal/battery materials demand spills beyond incumbents.
verdicts.neutral: The +3.55% day move is overhang-clearing after the June 8 share delivery rather than new fundamentals; YTD +8.8% is modest and the latent-node thesis remains speculative with medium evidence, so rank 51 still fits.
verdicts.bear: The majority sale of Silicones to Bluestar removes the most plausible AI-adjacent pathway (thermal/sealing materials), Q1 2026 showed production woes, and the equity raise plus weak silicon-market pricing signal a company shoring up its balance sheet, not riding an AI bottleneck.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Closing terms and use-of-proceeds clarity on the Bluestar silicones transaction, plus Q2 2026 results showing whether the retained silicon/carbon business has any AI-linked demand traction.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Thesis decay: post-divestiture Elkem is a cyclical ferrosilicon/foundry-alloys producer with no credible AI exposure, leaving the listing stranded on this page.
